what: Plot to Compare Two Sets of Estimates — compare_estimates
title: Plot to Compare Two Sets of Estimates — compare_estimates
text: function compares two sets estimates creating plot overlays estimates confidence intervals sets. can also display estimates two separate plots.

title: Plot to Compare Two Sets of Estimates — compare_estimates
code: compare_estimates(   tbl1,   tbl2,   title1 = NULL,   title2 = NULL,   title = NULL,   sig = 0.05,   colors = c("maroon", "blue4"),   x_title = "Coefficient (95% CI)",   y_title = "Variant",   axis_label_size = 9,   single_plot = TRUE,   pd = position_dodge(width = 0.8),   marker_order = NULL )

title: Plot to Compare Two Sets of Estimates — compare_estimates
text: tbl1 tibble containing first set summary statistics (e.g., coefficients, p-values, CI) variant. Expected columns : marker: name marker (e.g., variable name). pval: p-value marker. ci.lower: lower bound confidence interval. ci.upper: upper bound confidence interval. est: estimated coefficient. tbl2 tibble containing second set summary statistics variant (format tbl1). title1 Title tbl1 data. single_plot, legend label tbl1 data; otherwise title tbl1 plot. title2 Title tbl2 data. single_plot, legend label tbl2 data; otherwise title tbl2 plot. title (optional) main title combined plot, single_plot TRUE. sig (optional) individual plots, p-value threshold data points highlight significant. Defaults 0.05. colors (optional) combined plot, vector two colors represent two input datasets. x_title (optional) title x-axis. Defaults "Coefficient (95% CI)". y_title (optional) title y-axis. Defaults "Variant". axis_label_size (optional) font size axis labels. Defaults 9. single_plot boolean indicating whether make single combined plot (TRUE), plot dataset side--side (FALSE). pd (optional) Position dodge, .e. spacing 2 estimates positioned /line. Default 'position_dodge(width = 0.8)' marker_order (optional) Vector indicating order markers plotted y-axis.

title: Plot to Compare Two Sets of Estimates — compare_estimates
text: ggplot object displaying comparison two sets estimates.

what: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
text: Compares genotypes (presence resistance markers) observed phenotypes (R vs S, /NWT vs NWT) using binary matrix get_binary_matrix(). genotypic prediction variable defined basis genotype marker data (based variety possible rules including //minimum number markers; specifically markers combinations exceeding threshold positive predictive value (PPV);  predictions logistic regression model; user-defined field providing predictions). genotypic prediction compared observed phenotypes using standard classification metrics (via yardstick pkg) AMR-specific error rates (major error, major error, VME) per ISO 20776-2 (see FDA definitions). Supports evaluating R NWT outcomes single call, flexible prediction rules marker inclusion options.

title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
code: concordance(   binary_matrix,   markers = NULL,   exclude_markers = NULL,   ppv_threshold = NULL,   solo_ppv_results = NULL,   ppv_results = NULL,   prediction_col = NULL,   truth = c("R", "NWT"),   prediction_rule = "any",   min_count = NULL,   logreg_results = NULL,   pval_threshold = NULL )

title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
text: binary_matrix data frame output get_binary_matrix(), containing one row per sample, columns indicating binary phenotypes (R, , NWT) binary marker presence/absence. markers character vector marker column names include new binary outcome variable genotypic_prediction. Default NULL includes marker columns. exclude_markers character vector marker column names exclude genotypic prediction. Applied markers filtering. ppv_threshold numeric PPV threshold (0-1). Used solo PPV-based marker filtering solo_ppv_results provided, combination PPV threshold prediction_rule = "combo_ppv". solo_ppv_results Output solo_ppv(), used PPV-based marker filtering ppv_threshold set. ppv_results Output ppv(), required prediction_rule = "combo_ppv". summary table object used identify marker combinations PPV >= ppv_threshold relevant outcome (R.ppv NWT.ppv). Samples whose marker combination matches passing combination predicted positive. prediction_col character string naming column binary_matrix contains user-defined prediction (coded 0/1). supplied, marker filtering prediction generation bypassed; specified column used directly prediction outcomes truth. allows arbitrary prediction logic evaluated using concordance metrics. truth character vector specifying phenotypic truth column(s) evaluate: "R" (resistant vs susceptible/intermediate), "NWT" (non-wildtype vs wildtype), c("R", "NWT") (default) evaluate . prediction_rule rule generating genotypic predictions: "" (default) predicts positive marker present (applying filters specified markers, exclude_markers, ppv_threshold, pval_threshold, min_count); "" predicts positive markers present (applying filters specified markers, exclude_markers, ppv_threshold, pval_threshold, min_count); positive integer predicts positive least many markers present (applying filters specified markers, exclude_markers, ppv_threshold, pval_threshold, min_count); "logistic" uses logistic regression model logreg_results predict outcomes sample; "combo_ppv" predicts positive sample's marker combination PPV >= ppv_threshold ppv_results. min_count integer NULL. Exclude markers total frequency (column sum binary_matrix) value. Default NULL (filtering). logreg_results Output amr_logistic(). Used p-value filtering (pval_threshold set) prediction_rule = "logistic". pval_threshold numeric p-value threshold. Exclude markers logistic regression p-value >= value. Requires logreg_results.

title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
text: S3 object class "amr_concordance", list containing: conf_mat: Named list yardstick confusion matrix objects (e.g. list(R = <cm>, NWT = <cm>)). metrics: tibble columns outcome, metric, estimate. data: input binary matrix added R_pred /NWT_pred columns. markers_used: Named list character vectors markers used per outcome. truth_col: Character vector truth columns evaluated. n: Named integer vector sample counts per outcome. prediction_rule: prediction rule used.

title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
text: function identifies marker columns columns reserved set (id, pheno, ecoff, R, , NWT, mic, disk). applies filtering order: inclusion list markers, exclusion list exclude_markers, min_count, ppv_threshold filtering, pval_threshold filtering. Marker filtering performed per outcome (R, NWT), since PPV-based p-value-based filters category/outcome-specific. prediction_col supplied, marker filtering prediction generation bypassed. named column (0/1-coded) used directly genotypic prediction. useful user computed custom prediction (e.g. based marker combinations ppv()) wants evaluate concordance metrics truth columns. prediction_rule = "combo_ppv", function calls get_combo_matrix() internally derive combination identifier sample. Samples whose combination identifier matches entry ppv_results$summary outcome PPV >= ppv_threshold predicted positive. unique individual markers contributing passing combinations reported markers_used. Standard metrics (sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, accuracy, kappa, F-measure) calculated using pkg yardstick. AMR-specific error rates computed internally: VME (Major Error): FN / (TP + FN) = 1 - sensitivity. Proportion truly resistant isolates predicted genotype. (Major Error): FP / (TN + FP) = 1 - specificity. Proportion truly susceptible isolates incorrectly predicted resistant genotype.

title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ geno_table <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name")  binary_matrix <- get_binary_matrix(   geno_table = geno_table,   pheno_table = ecoli_pheno,   pheno_drug = "Ciprofloxacin",   geno_class = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "pheno_clsi" )  # Basic concordance for both R and NWT result <- concordance(binary_matrix) result  # Single outcome result <- concordance(binary_matrix, truth = "R")  # Exclude specific markers result <- concordance(binary_matrix, exclude_markers = c("qnrS1"))  # Filter markers by solo PPV threshold solo_ppv <- solo_ppv(binary_matrix = binary_matrix) result <- concordance(   binary_matrix,   ppv_threshold = 0.5,   solo_ppv_results = solo_ppv )  # Require at least 2 markers present for prediction result <- concordance(binary_matrix, prediction_rule = 2)  # Use logistic regression model for prediction logreg <- amr_logistic(binary_matrix = binary_matrix) result <- concordance(   binary_matrix,   prediction_rule = "logistic",   logreg_results = logreg )  # Predict based on marker combinations with PPV >= 0.5 (from ppv()) ppv_res <- ppv(binary_matrix = binary_matrix) result <- concordance(   binary_matrix,   prediction_rule = "combo_ppv",   ppv_results = ppv_res,   ppv_threshold = 0.5 )  # Use a custom user-defined prediction column binary_matrix$my_pred <- as.integer(binary_matrix$gyrA_S83L == 1 | binary_matrix$gyrA_D87N == 1) result <- concordance(binary_matrix, prediction_col = "my_pred", truth = "R")  # Access components result$conf_mat$R result$metrics result$markers_used  # predictions vs observed SIR calls result$data %>% count(R_pred, pheno) } # }

title: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
code: download_ebi(   data = "phenotype",   pheno_drug = NULL,   force_drug_name = FALSE,   genus = NULL,   species = NULL,   geno_subclass = NULL,   geno_class = NULL,   geno_drug = NULL,   remove_dup = FALSE,   release = NULL,   reformat = FALSE,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
text: data String specifying type data download, either "phenotype" "genotype" (default "phenotype"). pheno_drug (Optional) String (vector strings) specifying drug name/s filter phenotype data (default NULL). Uses AMR package try fix typos, format lower-case EBI files. used data = "phenotype". force_drug_name (Optional) Logical indicating whether turn parsing drug names match exactly input strings (default FALSE). genus (Optional) String specifying bacterial genus filter (default NULL, pull taxa). species (Optional) String specifying bacterial species filter (default NULL, pull taxa). used genus specified. geno_subclass (Optional) String specifying antibiotic subclass filter genotype data (default NULL). Filter based string match, identity, e.g. subclass="TRIMETHOPRIM" return rows string "TRIMETHOPRIM" included subclass field. used data="genotype". Check NCBI AMR Class-Subclass Reference valid terms. geno_class (Optional) String specifying antibiotic subclass filter genotype data (default NULL``). Filter based string match, identity, e.g. class="TRIMETHOPRIM" return rows string "TRIMETHOPRIM" included class field. used data`="genotype" subclass specified. Check NCBI AMR Class-Subclass Reference valid terms. geno_drug (Optional) String (vector strings) specifying drug name/s filter (default NULL). Filter based valid CARD terms EBI antibiotic_name field used data = "genotype" neither geno_subclass geno_class specified. remove_dup (Optional) Logical specifying whether clean genotype data removing duplicates hit (default FALSE). detected gene associated subclass value actually list multiple drugs/classes, e.g. subclass="GENTAMICIN/TOBRAMYCIN", EBI data table duplicate rows gene hit, different values antibiotic_name associated antibiotic_ontology antibiotic_ontology_link annotation fields (e.g. one row gentamicin tobramycin). remove duplicate rows (drug-specific annotation fields) return one row per hit (.e. restoring AMRFinderPlus output format), set TRUE. release (Optional) String specifying data release download (default NULL, pull latest release). reformat (Optional) Logical specifying whether reformat downloaded data easy use downstream AMRgen functions, using import_ebi_pheno_ftp (phenotypes) import_amrfp_ebi_ftp. Default FALSE. things like format antibiotic, measurement, phenotype columns AMR package classes. set TRUE can also turn re-interpreting MIC/disk data using latest EUCAST/CLSI breakpoints (data="phenotype"). columns removed downloaded data frame, key fields renamed, see documentation format_pheno import_amrfp_ebi_ftp. interpret_eucast (Optional) Logical specifying whether re-interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class sir. used downloading phenotype data, reformat set TRUE. interpret_clsi (Optional) Logical specifying whether re-interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class sir. used downloading phenotype data, reformat set TRUE. interpret_ecoff (Optional) Logical specifying whether re-interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class sir coded R (nonwildtype) S (wildtype). used downloading phenotype data, reformat set TRUE.

title: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
text: data frame containing phenotype genotype data retrieved EBI, optionally reformatted AMRgen standard formats classes.

title: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
text: See https://www.ebi.ac.uk/amr// information available portal, https://github.com/ncbi/amr/wiki/class-subclass valid class subclass terms. Note function downloads full genotype phenotype data table filtering provided parameters, trouble drug/class names matching just run without specifying genus/species/drug/class filters, get full unfiltered table explore field values filter manually get want.

title: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # download all phenotype data from EBI pheno_ebi <- download_ebi()  # download phenotype data from Dec 2025 release, and filter to Salmonella pheno_salmonella <- download_ebi(   genus = "Salmonella",   release = "2025-12" )  # reformat downloaded phenotype data to simplify use with AMRgen functions pheno_salmonella <- import_ebi_pheno_ftp(pheno_salmonella)   # download phenotype data for Salmonella, filter to ampicillin and ciprofloxacin pheno_salmonella <- download_ebi(   genus = "Salmonella",   pheno_drug = c("ampicillin", "Cipro") )  # download phenotype data for Staphylococcus aureus and reformat # for use with AMRgen functions pheno_staph <- download_ebi(   species = "Staphylococcus aureus",   reformat = T )  # download phenotype data for Klebsiella quasipneumoniae, reformat # for use with AMRgen functions, and re-interpret phenotypes pheno_kquasi_reinterpreted <- download_ebi(   species = "Klebsiella quasipneumoniae",   reformat = T,   interpret_eucast = TRUE,   interpret_clsi = TRUE,   interpret_ecoff = TRUE )  # download all available genotype data ebi_geno <- download_ebi(data = "genotype")  # download genotype data for Klebsiella pneumoniae, and filter to # markers assigned to NCBI class 'TRIMETHOPRIM' geno_kpn_tmp <- download_ebi(   data = "genotype",   species = "Klebsiella pneumoniae",   geno_subclass = "TRIMETHOPRIM" )  # download genotype data for Klebsiella pneumoniae, and filter to # markers assigned to NCBI class 'TRIMETHOPRIM' or 'QUINOLONE' geno_kpn_tmp <- download_ebi(   data = "genotype",   species = "Klebsiella pneumoniae",   geno_subclass = c("TRIMETHOPRIM", "QUINOLONE") )  # download genotype data for Klebsiella pneumoniae, and filter to # markers associated with CARD drug term 'trimethoprim' geno_kpn_tmp <- download_ebi(   data = "genotype",   species = "Klebsiella pneumoniae",   geno_drug = "trimethoprim" ) } # }

title: Download NCBI antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data — download_ncbi_pheno
code: download_ncbi_pheno(   species,   pheno_drug = NULL,   max_records = 15000,   batch_size = 200,   sleep_time = 0.34,   force_drug_name = FALSE,   reformat = FALSE,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Download NCBI antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data — download_ncbi_pheno
text: species Character. Organism name search query (e.g., "Salmonella enterica"). Required. pheno_drug Character vector. Optional drug name/s filter returned data. Strings processed using AMR package standardize names matching, e.g. "amikacin" "Amikacin" "ami" parsed "amikacin" matching. can turned setting force_drug_name=TRUE. Full list allowed drug names NCBI: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/biosample/docs/antibiogram/. max_records Integer. Maximum number BioSample records retrieve. Default 15000. batch_size Integer. Number records fetched per API request. Default 200 recommended NCBI. sleep_time Numeric. Seconds pause batch requests avoid overloading NCBI servers. Default 0.34. force_drug_name Logical. TRUE, turns standardizing drug name using AMR package filtering, matching done exactly input string/s. Default FALSE. reformat Logical. TRUE, reformats output using import_ncbi_biosample() compatibility AMR analysis workflows. Default FALSE. set TRUE, data can also interpreted breakpoints/ECOFF setting interpret_*=TRUE. interpret_eucast Logical. Passed import_ncbi_biosample(). TRUE, interprets MIC values using EUCAST breakpoints. Default FALSE. used reformat=TRUE. interpret_clsi Logical. Passed import_ncbi_biosample(). TRUE, interprets MIC values using CLSI breakpoints. Default FALSE. used reformat=TRUE. interpret_ecoff Logical. Passed import_ncbi_biosample(). TRUE, interprets MIC values using ECOFF cutoffs. Default FALSE. used reformat=TRUE.

See import_ncbi_biosample() details output formats options used.

text: eurogasp_geno_raw data frame 54,764 rows 24 columns: Name: Sample identifier (ENA run accession). Protein identifier: Protein identifier (used N. gonorrhoeae assemblies; NA). Contig id: Assembly contig identifier. Start, Stop: Start stop positions AMR element contig. Strand: Strand orientation (+ -). Element symbol: AMRFinderPlus element symbol (gene name /mutation, e.g. gyrA_S91F). Sequence name: Full descriptive name AMR element. Scope: AMRFinderPlus scope (core plus). Element type: Type AMR element (e.g. AMR). Element subtype: Subtype AMR element (e.g. POINT, AMR). Class: Antibiotic class (e.g. QUINOLONE, BETA-LACTAM). Subclass: Antibiotic subclass (e.g. CEPHALOSPORIN, TETRACYCLINE). Method: Detection method used AMRFinderPlus (e.g. POINTX, BLASTX). Target length, Reference sequence length, Alignment length: Sequence length metrics (bp). % Coverage reference sequence: Percentage reference sequence covered alignment. % Identity reference sequence: Percentage identity closest reference sequence. Accession closest sequence: NCBI accession closest reference sequence. Name closest sequence: Name closest reference sequence. HMM id, HMM description: HMM-based annotation fields (NA dataset). Hierarchy node: Gene hierarchy node name, required import_amrfp (enabled --print_node flag).

title: Export EBI Antibiogram — export_ebi_pheno
text: data data frame AMRgen long format (e.g. output import_pheno() format_pheno()). Expected columns: id, drug, spp_pheno, least one phenotype column (see pheno_col). Optional columns: mic, disk, method, platform. pheno_col Character string naming column contains SIR interpretations (class sir). Default "pheno_provided". guideline Optional character string record ast_standard field output (default NULL, case ast_standard populated guideline field input file). breakpoint_version Character string specifying breakpoint version used interpretation (e.g. "EUCAST 2024"). Default NULL. submission_account Character string specifying EBI Webin submission account identifier (e.g. "Webin-###"). provided, JSON output files generated function return formated table , can updated converted submission-ready JSON later using format_ebi_json(). domain Character string specifying domain used submission metadata (e.g. "self.ExampleDomain"). provided, JSON output files generated function return formated table , can updated converted submission-ready JSON later using format_ebi_json(). output_dir Character string specifying directory JSON files written. provided, JSON output files generated function return formated table , can updated converted submission-ready JSON later using format_ebi_json().

what: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from a generic format — format_pheno
title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from a generic format — format_pheno
text: function attempts import antibiotic susceptibility testing (AST) data long-form antibiogram format (one row per sample test), suitable downstream use AMRgen analysis functions. assumes input file tab-delimited text file (e.g., TSV) CSV (may compressed) parses relevant columns (antibiotic names, species names, MIC disk data, S//R calls) suitable classes using AMR package. optionally can use AMR package interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) based EUCAST CLSI guidelines (human breakpoints /ECOFF).

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from a generic format — format_pheno
code: format_pheno(   input,   sample_col = "id",   species = NULL,   species_col = "spp_pheno",   ab = NULL,   ab_col = "drug",   mic_col = "mic",   disk_col = "disk",   pheno_cols = c("ecoff", "pheno_eucast", "pheno_clsi", "pheno_provided"),   method_col = "method",   platform_col = "platform",   source_col = "source",   guideline_col = "guideline",   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE,   rename_cols = TRUE )

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from a generic format — format_pheno
text: input string representing dataframe, path input file, containing AST data long-form antibiogram format (one row per sample test). might file containing content EBI/NCBI AST dataset previously processed using import_ebi_pheno(), import_ncbi_pheno(), import_pheno() functions, files similar format/structure different column names. sample_col (optional, default "id") String indicating name input data column provides sample name. rename_cols parameter set TRUE, column renamed id. species (optional, default NULL) String indicating name single species samples belong. Use want interpret assay measurements input file contain column indicating species sample (called species name specified species_col parameter). species_col (optional, default "species") String indicating name input data column provides species name. provided, column converted micro-organism class mo via AMR::.mo(). rename_cols parameter set TRUE, column also renamed spp_pheno. interpretation switched , column used identify appropriate  breakpoints interpretation row data table. ab (optional, default NULL) String indicating name single antibiotic use phenotype interpretation. Use want interpret assay measurements input file contain column indicating drug sample (called drug name specified ab_col parameter). ab_col (optional, default "drug") String indicating name input data column provides drug name. provided, column converted antibiotic class ab via AMR::.ab(). rename_cols parameter set TRUE, column also renamed drug. interpretation switched , column used identify appropriate breakpoints interpretation row data table. mic_col (optional, default "mic") String indicating name input data column provides MIC measurements. provided, column converted MIC class mic via AMR::.mic(). rename_cols parameter set TRUE, column also renamed mic. interpretation switched , MIC values interpreted clinical breakpoints. disk_col (optional, default "disk") String indicating name input data column provides disk diffusion zone measurements. provided, column converted disk diffusion class disk via AMR::.disk(). rename_cols parameter set TRUE, column also renamed disk. interpretation switched , zone values interpreted clinical breakpoints. pheno_cols (optional, default c("ecoff", "pheno_eucast", "pheno_clsi", "pheno_provided")) String, vector strings, indicating name/s input data column/s provide interpreted phenotypes (column values encoded S//R WT/NWT). provided, columns converted class sir via AMR::.sir(). method_col (optional, default "method") String indicating name input data column indicates testing method used (e.g. "broth dilution", "disk diffusion"). rename_cols parameter set TRUE, column also renamed method. platform_col (optional, default "platform") String indicating name input data column indicates testing platform used (e.g. "Vitek", "Sensititre"). rename_cols parameter set TRUE, column also renamed platform. source_col (optional, default "source") String indicating name input data column indicates source dataset (e.g. "BioProject", "PMID"). rename_cols parameter set TRUE, column also renamed source. guideline_col (optional, default "guideline") String indicating name input data column indicates guideline used testing (e.g. "EUCAST", "CLSI"). rename_cols parameter set TRUE, column also renamed guideline. interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class sir. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class 'sir'. interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class sir coded NWT (nonwildtype) WT (wildtype). rename_cols logical value (default TRUE). TRUE, function rename provided columns (specified sample_col, ab_col, mic_col, disk_col, species_col) default names expected AMRgen functions (id, drug, mic, disk, spp_pheno), match output import_pheno() functions.

Default "human" returns human clinical breakpoints, change "ECOFF" get epidemiological cutoff.

what: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
text: function generates binary matrix representing resistance (R vs S/) nonwildtype (NWT vs WT, R/vs S) status given drug, presence absence genetic markers related one specified drugs classes. takes input separate tables genotype phenotype data, matches according common identifier (either specified column names assuming first column contains ID), filters data according specified phenotype genotype drug criteria creating binary matrix. Suitable input files can generated using import_pheno() import phenotype data, import_geno() import genotype data AMRFinderPlus genotypers.

title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
code: get_binary_matrix(   geno_table,   pheno_table,   pheno_drug = NULL,   geno_class = NULL,   geno_drug = NULL,   keep_SIR = TRUE,   keep_assay_values = FALSE,   keep_assay_values_from = c("mic", "disk"),   geno_sample_col = NULL,   pheno_sample_col = NULL,   sir_col = "pheno_clsi",   ecoff_col = "ecoff",   marker_col = "marker",   most_resistant = TRUE )

title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
text: geno_table data frame containing genotype data, long form one row per sample genetic marker. Expected format output import_amrfp() must include column labeled drug_class (indicating antibiotic class associated marker), addition column indicating marker (column name specified via marker_col) column sample identifiers (specified via geno_sample_col, otherwise assumed first column contains identifiers). pheno_table data frame containing phenotype data, long form one row per sample, drug assay result. Expected format output import_pheno() must include column drug (indicating drug, interpretable AMR pkg class ab), addition column sample identifiers (specified via pheno_sample_col, otherwise assumed first column contains identifiers), column resistance interpretation (S//R, specified via sir_col), optionally column ECOFF interpretation (WT/NWT S/R, specified via ecoff_col). pheno_drug character string specifying drug interest filter phenotype data. value must match one entries drug column pheno_table coercible match using AMR::.ab. geno_class (Optional) character vector drug classes filter genotype markers. Markers geno_table filtered based whether drug_class matches value list. provided, AMR pkg used check class name/s associated pheno_drug uses (printed screen user can see filtered). geno_drug (Optional) character vector drug names whose relevant genotype markers included. keep_SIR logical indicating whether retain full S//R phenotype column output. Defaults TRUE. keep_assay_values logical indicating whether include columns raw phenotype assay data. Assumes columns labelled "mic" "disk"; added output table present. Defaults FALSE. keep_assay_values_from character vector specifying assay values (e.g., "mic", "disk") retain keep_assay_values TRUE. Defaults c("mic", "disk"). geno_sample_col (Optional) character string specifying column name geno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. pheno_sample_col (Optional) character string specifying column name pheno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. sir_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretation (SIR) data. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). provided, first column prefixed "phenotype*" used present, otherwise error thrown.  used binary_matrix provided. ecoff_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains ECOFF interpretation phenotype. values interpretable "WT" (wildtype) "NWT" (nonwildtype), "S" / "" / "R". Default "ecoff. marker_col character string specifying column name geno_table containing marker identifiers. Defaults "marker". most_resistant logical indicating whether, multiple phenotype entries present sample drug, resistant kept (otherwise least resistant kept). Default TRUE.

title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
text: data frame row represents sample, column represents genetic marker related specified drug's genotype class drug mapping. binary values matrix indicate presence (1) absence (0) marker sample, along resistance status columns specified drug: R resistant (defined sir_col, 1=R, 0=/S) NWT nonwildtype (defined ecoff_col provided: 1=NWT, 0=WT; otherwise defined sir_col: 1=/R, 0=S).

title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
text: function performs several steps: Verifies pheno_table contains drug column converts class ab necessary. Filters pheno_table retain data related specified phenotype drug. Checks geno_table contains markers associated specified genotype class(es) /drug(s). Matches sample identifiers geno_table pheno_table. Extracts transforms phenotype data binary format indicating resistance NWT status. Constructs binary matrix genotype data, column representing genetic marker. Returns single matrix sample identifiers plus binary variables phenotype genetic marker.

title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Import example E. coli AMRFinderPlus data from AllTheBacteria ecoli_geno <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name") geno_pheno_cip <- get_binary_matrix(   ecoli_geno,   ecoli_pheno,   pheno_drug = "Ciprofloxacin",   geno_class = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "pheno_clsi" ) geno_pheno_cip <- get_binary_matrix(   ecoli_geno,   ecoli_pheno,   pheno_drug = "Ciprofloxacin",   geno_class = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "pheno_provided",   keep_assay_values = TRUE ) } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ get_eucast_mic_distribution("cipro")  # not returning as frequency table get_eucast_mic_distribution("cipro", as_freq_table = FALSE)  # specify microorganism to only get results for that pathogen get_eucast_mic_distribution("cipro", "K. pneumoniae")  get_eucast_disk_distribution("cipro", "K. pneumoniae")   # Plotting ----------------------------------------------------------------  mic_data <- get_eucast_mic_distribution("cipro", "K. pneumoniae") mics <- rep(mic_data$mic, mic_data$count) ggplot2::autoplot(mics, ab = "cipro", mo = "K. pneumoniae", title = "Look at my MICs!")   # Comparing With User Values ----------------------------------------------  my_mic_values <- AMR::random_mic(500) comparison <- compare_mic_with_eucast(my_mic_values, ab = "cipro", mo = "K. pneumoniae") comparison ggplot2::autoplot(comparison) } # }

title: Extract Details from a Generalised Linear Model — glm_details
code: # Generate example data set.seed(1) dat <- data.frame(   R = rbinom(100, 1, 0.3),   geneA = rbinom(100, 1, 0.2),   geneB = rbinom(100, 1, 0.5),   geneC = rbinom(100, 1, 0.3) )  # Fit logistic regression model and extract details model <- glm(R ~ ., data = dat, family = binomial(link = "logit")) model_details <- glm_details(model) #> Waiting for profiling to be done...  # Plot model summary ggplot2::autoplot(model_details)

title: Import and Process ABRicate Results — import_abricate

title: Import and Process ABRicate Results — import_abricate
text: input_table character string specifying dataframe path ABRicate results table. sample_col character string specifying column identifies samples dataset (default "FILE"). gene_col character string specifying column identifies gene symbols dataset (default "GENE"). product_col character string specifying column identifies product names dataset (default "PRODUCT"). ab_col character string specifying column identifies drug/s detected gene associated (default "RESISTANCE"). db character string specifying AMR gene database ABRicate run (default "resfinder"; "ncbi" also supported).

title: Import and Process ABRicate Results — import_abricate
text: data frame processed genotype data, harmonised gene names, mapped drug agents, drug classes can used functions ARMgen package: id: sample identifier (character). marker: name genotype marker, appears GENE column input file (character). gene: name gene product, appears PRODUCT column input file (character). drug_class: Name antibiotic group associated genotype marker, compatible AMR pkg, parsed RESISTANCE column input file depends database ABRicate run (character). drug: Name specific antibiotic associated genotype marker, compatible AMR pkg, parsed RESISTANCE column input file (ab). Value NA assigned markers annotated class specific antibiotic. variation type: Type variation, .e. Gene presence detected, ABRicate detects presence/absence genes query database. ... fields specific input file

title: Import and Process ABRicate Results — import_abricate

Converts drug agent names classes terms recognised AMR package.

what: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp

title: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp
code: import_amrfp(   input_table,   sample_col = "Name",   element_symbol_col = NULL,   element_type_col = NULL,   element_subtype_col = "Element subtype",   method_col = "Method",   node_col = "Hierarchy node",   subclass_col = "Subclass",   class_col = "Class",   amrfp_drugs = amrfp_drugs_table )

title: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp
text: input_table character string specifying dataframe path AMRFinderPlus results table (TSV format). sample_col character string specifying column identifies samples dataset (default "Name"). element_symbol_col Optional character string specifying column containing gene element symbols non-standard column names used. element_type_col Optional character string specifying column indicating element type (e.g. AMR). element_subtype_col Character string specifying column used detect mutation subtypes. method_col Character string specifying AMRFinderPlus method column. node_col Character string specifying hierarchy node column. subclass_col Character string specifying AMRFinderPlus subclass column. class_col Character string specifying AMRFinderPlus class column. amrfp_drugs tibble containing reference table mapping AMRFinderPlus subclasses (AMRFP_Subclass) standardised drug classes (drug_class). Defaults amrfp_drugs_table, provided internally.

title: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp
text: data frame processed genotype data, harmonised gene names, mapped drug agents, drug classes can used functions ARMgen package. output retains original columns AMRFinderPlus table along newly mapped variables: id: sample identifier (character). marker: name genotype marker appears input (e.g. gyrA_S83F) (character). gene: gene identifier (character). mutation: mutation detected within gene, converted HGVS nomenclature syntax (e.g. Ser83Phe) (character). node: node NCBI reference gene hierarchy corresponding gene (character). drug_class: Name antibiotic group associated genotype marker, compatible AMR pkg (character). drug: Name specific antibiotic associated genotype marker, compatible AMR pkg (ab). Value NA assigned markers annotated class specific antibiotic. variation type: Type variation, e.g. Gene presence detected, Protein variant detected, Nucleotide variant detected, Inactivating mutation detected, Promoter variant detected. ... fields specific input file

title: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp
text: function performs following steps: Reads AMRFinderPlus output table. Filters data include AMR elements. Converts gene symbols harmonised format. Splits multiple subclass annotations separate rows. Maps AMRFinderPlus subclasses standardised drug class names recognised AMR pkg. processing ensures compatibility downstream AMRgen analysis workflows.

title: Import EBI-processed AMRFinderPlus Genotypes — import_amrfp_ebi
text: data pre-processed EBI match NCBI class/subclass CARD's antibiotic resistance ontology (ARO), however consistency function re-process data generate drug drug_class fields consistent import_amrfp() function (EBI fields antibiotic* also retained). Note several AMRFinderPlus fields excluded EBI files, including hierarchy node, method, percent identity coverage; therefore unlike import_amrfp() function, function assign variation type node. function performs following steps: Reads EBI-processed genotype table. Maps AMRFinderPlus subclasses standardised drug agent drug class names using amrfp_drugs (EBI-mappings retained antibiotic* fields.) Converts drug agent names ab class AMR package. processing ensures compatibility downstream AMR analysis workflows.

what: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_pheno
title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_pheno
text: function imports antibiotic susceptibility testing (AST) dataset downloaded EBI AMR portal website (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/amr/data/?view=experiments) Data downloaded EBI AMR Portal FTP site (ftp://ftp.ebi.ac.uk/pub/databases/amr_portal/releases/), either directly via function download_ebi(), formatted differently can instead processed using import_ebi_pheno_ftp() function.

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_pheno
code: import_ebi_pheno(   input,   sample_col = "phenotype-BioSample_ID",   source = NULL,   species = NULL,   ab = NULL,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_pheno
text: input string representing dataframe, path input file, containing AST data EBI antibiogram format. files can downloaded EBI AMR browser, e.g. https://www.ebi.ac.uk/amr/data/?view=experiments. sample_col string indicating name column sample identifiers. NULL, assume "phenotype-BioSample_ID". source (optional) single string record source data points, e.g. "EBI_browser". default, field phenotype-AMR_associated_publications used indicate source row input file, missing want override single value samples, may provide source name via parameter. species (optional) Name species use phenotype interpretation. default, field phenotype-organism assumed specify species row input file, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single species name via parameter. ab (optional) Name antibiotic use phenotype interpretation. default, field phenotype-antibiotic_name assumed specify antibiotic row input file, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single antibiotic name via parameter. interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class sir. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class sir. interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class sir coded NWT (nonwildtype) WT (wildtype).

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_pheno
text: data frame processed AST data, including additional columns: id: biological sample identifier (renamed phenotype-BioSample_ID specified column). spp_pheno: species phenotype, formatted using AMR::.mo() function. drug: antibiotic used test, formatted using AMR::.ab() function. mic: minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) value, formatted using AMR::.mic() function. disk: disk diffusion measurement (mm), formatted using AMR::.disk() function. method: AST method (e.g., "broth dilution", "disk diffusion", "Etest", "agar dilution"). Expected values based NCBI/EBI antibiogram specification. platform: AST platform/instrument (e.g., "Vitek", "Phoenix", "Sensititre"). guideline: AST standard recorded input file used AST assay. pheno_eucast: phenotype newly interpreted EUCAST human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_clsi: phenotype newly interpreted CLSI human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. ecoff: phenotype newly interpreted ECOFF (WT/NWT), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_provided: original phenotype interpretation provided input file. source: source data point (renamed publications field input file, replaced single value passed source parameter).

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_pheno
text: function process data, optionally interpret results based MIC disk diffusion data. assumes input file tab-delimited text file (e.g., TSV) CSV (may compressed) parses relevant columns (antibiotic names, species names, MIC disk measures) suitable classes using AMR package. optionally can use AMR package interpret MIC/disk zone measures susceptibility phenotypes (SIR) based EUCAST CLSI guidelines (human breakpoints /ECOFF).

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_pheno
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # import without re-interpreting resistance pheno <- import_ebi_pheno("EBI_AMR_data.csv.gz") head(pheno)  # import and re-interpret resistance (S/I/R) and WT/NWT (vs ECOFF) using AMR package pheno <- import_ebi_pheno("EBI_AMR_data.csv.gz", interpret_eucast = TRUE, interpret_ecoff = TRUE) head(pheno) } # }

  `HMM id` <lgl>, `HMM description` <lgl>, `Hierarchy node` <chr>, … geno <- import_geno(ecoli_geno_raw %>% head(n = 10), format = "amrfp") head(geno) #> # A tibble: 6 × 37 #>   id           marker     gene  mutation drug drug_class  `variation type` node  #>   <chr>        <chr>      <chr> <chr>    <ab> <chr>       <chr>            <chr> #> 1 SAMN03177615 blaEC      blaEC NA       NA   Beta-lacta… Gene presence d… blaEC #> 2 SAMN03177615 acrF       acrF  NA       NA   Efflux      Gene presence d… acrF  #> 3 SAMN03177615 glpT_E448K glpT  Glu448L… FOS  Phosphonics Protein variant… glpT  #> 4 SAMN03177615 floR       floR  NA       CHL  Phenicols   Gene presence d… floR  #> 5 SAMN03177615 floR       floR  NA       FLR  Phenicols   Gene presence d… floR  #> 6 SAMN03177615 mdtM       mdtM  NA       NA   Efflux      Gene presence d… mdtM  #> # ℹ 29 more variables: marker.label <chr>, `Protein identifier` <lgl>, #> #   `Contig id` <chr>, Start <dbl>, Stop <dbl>, Strand <chr>, #> #   `Gene symbol` <chr>, `Sequence name` <chr>, Scope <chr>, #> #   `Element type` <chr>, `Element subtype` <chr>, Class <chr>, Subclass <chr>, #> #   Method <chr>, `Target length` <dbl>, `Reference sequence length` <dbl>, #> #   `% Coverage of reference sequence` <dbl>, #> #   `% Identity to reference sequence` <dbl>, `Alignment length` <dbl>, …  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Import ABRicate results (that were run using the default db, resfinder) abricate_resfinder <- import_geno("path/to/abricate_resfinder.tsv",   format = "abricate" )  # Import ABRicate results that were run using an alternative db (ncbi) abricate_ncbi <- import_geno("path/to/abricate_ncbi.tsv",   format = "abricate",   db = "ncbi" )  # Import Kleborate results kleborate_geno <- import_geno(kleborate_raw %>% head(n = 10),   format = "kleborate" )  # Import Kleborate results run with an older version without HGVS syntax kleborate_old <- import_geno(kleborate_raw_v313 %>% head(n = 10),   format = "kleborate",   hgvs = FALSE )  # Import CARD RGI results with default parameters rgi_geno <- import_geno(rgi_EuSCAPE_raw %>% head(n = 10),   format = "rgi" )  # Import CARD RGI results with additional options rgi_geno <- import_geno(rgi_raw %>% head(n = 10),   format = "rgi",   sample_id_sep = "_genomic.fna.txt:", exclude_loose = FALSE )  # Download quinolone-related genotype data for E. coli, from EBI ebi_geno_raw <- download_ebi(   data = "genotype", species = "Escherichia coli",   geno_subclass = "QUINOLONE" ) # import the downloaded data ebi_geno <- import_geno(ebi_geno_raw,   format = "ebi_ftp" )  # Download data from EBI web portal manually, and import the file ebi_geno_from_web <- import_geno("amr_records.csv",   format = "ebi_web" )  # Download carbapenem-related genotype data for K. pneumoniae, from NCBI ncbi_geno_raw <- query_ncbi_bq_geno(   taxgroup = "Klebsiella pneumoniae",   geno_subclass = "CARBAPENEM" ) # import the downloaded data geno <- import_geno(ncbi_geno_raw,   format = "amrfp",   sample_col = "biosample_acc" ) } # }

what: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
text: function imports processes genotyping results Kleborate (https://github.com/klebgenomics/Kleborate), extracting antimicrobial resistance determinants mapping standardised drug classes.

title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
code: import_kleborate(   input_table,   sample_col = "strain",   kleborate_class_table = kleborate_classes,   hgvs = TRUE )

title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
text: input_table character string specifying dataframe path Kleborate results table (TSV format). sample_col character string specifying column identifies samples dataset (default strain). kleborate_class_table tibble containing reference table mapping Kleborate drug class column names (Kleborate_Class) standardised drug classes (drug_class). Defaults kleborate_classes, provided internally. hgvs Logical indicating whether expect mutations HGVS nomenclature syntax (used Kleborate releases since v3.1.3). Default TRUE, expects mutations formatted e.g. "GyrA:p.S83F". Set FALSE results generated using older versions mutations formatted e.g. "GyrA_83F".

title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
text: data frame processed genotype data, harmonised gene names, mapped drug agents, drug classes can used functions ARMgen package: id: sample identifier (character). marker: name genotype marker appears input (e.g. GyrA:p.S83F recent versions Kleborate, GyrA-83F earlier versions using HGVS nomenclature) (character). gene: gene identifier (character). mutation: mutation detected within gene, converted HGVS nomenclature syntax (e.g. Ser83Phe) (character). drug_class: Name antibiotic group associated genotype marker, compatible AMR pkg, drawn Kleborate column marker reported (character). drug: Values recorded NA Kleborate report markers assigned individual drug level. variation type: Type variation, e.g. Gene presence detected, Protein variant detected, Nucleotide variant detected, Inactivating mutation detected. ... fields specific input file

title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
text: function performs following steps: Reads Kleborate output table. Transforms Kleborate output long form (.e., one AMR determinant per row). Maps Kleborate drug classes standardised drug class names recognised AMR pkg. processing ensures compatibility downstream AMRgen analysis workflows.

title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
code: # example Kleborate data from EUSCAPE project kleborate_raw #> # A tibble: 1,490 × 122 #>    strain    species species_match contig_count    N50 largest_contig total_size #>    <chr>     <chr>   <chr>                <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 SAMEA349… Klebsi… strong                 141 230759         470757    5578320 #>  2 SAMEA349… Klebsi… strong                  88 370309         938079    5384685 #>  3 SAMEA349… Klebsi… strong                  90 238750         529125    5446454 #>  4 SAMEA349… Klebsi… strong                 144 207582         663698    5574298 #>  5 SAMEA349… Klebsi… strong                 142 263498         678692    5486238 #>  6 SAMEA349… Klebsi… strong                  79 285199         991412    5529803 #>  7 SAMEA349… Klebsi… strong                 280 178980         585359    5817055 #>  8 SAMEA349… Klebsi… strong                 108 209418         517450    5379124 #>  9 SAMEA349… Klebsi… strong                 134 371444         984005    5558705 #> 10 SAMEA349… Klebsi… strong                 142 197944         636773    5497421 #> # ℹ 1,480 more rows #> # ℹ 115 more variables: GC_content <dbl>, ambiguous_bases <chr>, #> #   QC_warnings <chr>, ST <chr>, gapA <dbl>, infB <dbl>, mdh <dbl>, pgi <dbl>, #> #   phoE <dbl>, rpoB <dbl>, tonB <dbl>, YbST <chr>, Yersiniabactin <chr>, #> #   ybtS <chr>, ybtX <chr>, ybtQ <chr>, ybtP <chr>, ybtA <chr>, irp2 <chr>, #> #   irp1 <chr>, ybtU <chr>, ybtT <chr>, ybtE <chr>, fyuA <chr>, #> #   spurious_ybt_hits <chr>, CbST <chr>, Colibactin <chr>, clbA <chr>, …  # import first few rows of this data frame and parse it to standard genotype table format kleborate_geno <- import_kleborate(kleborate_raw %>% head(n = 10))  # parse the output of an older version of Kleborate (v3.1.3) before # HGVS syntax was introduced for mutations kleborate_geno <- import_kleborate(kleborate_raw_v313 %>% head(n = 10), hgvs = FALSE)

what: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from MicroScan instruments — import_microscan_pheno
title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from MicroScan instruments — import_microscan_pheno
text: function imports antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data MicroScan instrument output files (wide CSV format) converts standardised long-format used AMRgen. Supports English, Spanish, French, German, Portuguese column names (auto-detected metadata columns).

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from MicroScan instruments — import_microscan_pheno
code: import_microscan_pheno(   input,   sample_col = NULL,   source = NULL,   species = NULL,   ab = NULL,   instrument_guideline = NULL,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from MicroScan instruments — import_microscan_pheno
text: input dataframe path CSV/TSV file containing MicroScan AST output data sample_col String indicating name column containing sample identifiers. Default: "Lab ID" source Optional string value record source column data points (e.g., dataset name study identifier) species Optional string indicating single species use phenotype interpretation (otherwise inferred per-sample input) ab Optional string indicating single antibiotic use phenotype interpretation (otherwise inferred per-sample input) instrument_guideline Optional string indicating guideline used instrument SIR interpretation (e.g., "EUCAST 2025", "CLSI 2025"), used record guideline output file. Default: NULL interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function re-interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) observation based MIC values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class sir. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function re-interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) observation based MIC values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class sir. interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function re-interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status observation based MIC values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class sir coded NWT (nonwildtype) WT (wildtype).

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from MicroScan instruments — import_microscan_pheno
text: Standardised AST data frame

what: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the NCBI AST browser — import_ncbi_pheno
title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the NCBI AST browser — import_ncbi_pheno
text: function imports antibiotic susceptibility testing (AST) dataset, processes data, optionally interprets results based MIC disk diffusion data. assumes input file tab-delimited text file (e.g., TSV) CSV (may compressed) parses relevant columns (antibiotic names, species names, MIC disk data) suitable classes using AMR package. optionally can use AMR package interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) based EUCAST CLSI guidelines (human breakpoints /ECOFF).

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the NCBI AST browser — import_ncbi_pheno
code: import_ncbi_pheno(   input,   sample_col = "BioSample",   source = NULL,   species = NULL,   ab = NULL,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the NCBI AST browser — import_ncbi_pheno
text: input string representing dataframe, path input file, containing AST data NCBI antibiogram format. files can downloaded NCBI AST browser, e.g. https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pathogens/ast#Pseudomonas%20aeruginosa sample_col string indicating name column sample identifiers. NULL, assume "BioSample". source (optional) single string record source data points, e.g. "NCBI_browser". default, field BioProject used indicate source row input file, missing want override single value samples, may provide source name via parameter. species (optional) Name species use phenotype interpretation. default, field Scientific name assumed specify species row input file, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single species name via parameter. ab (optional) Name antibiotic use phenotype interpretation. default, field Antibiotic assumed specify antibiotic row input file, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single antibiotic name via parameter. interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class sir. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class sir. interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class sir coded NWT (nonwildtype) WT (wildtype).

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the NCBI AST browser — import_ncbi_pheno
text: data frame processed AST data, including additional columns: id: biological sample identifier (renamed BioSample specified column). spp_pheno: species phenotype, formatted using AMR::.mo() function. drug: antibiotic used test, formatted using AMR::.ab() function. mic: minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) value, formatted using AMR::.mic() function. disk: disk diffusion measurement (mm), formatted using AMR::.disk() function. method: AST method (e.g., "broth dilution", "disk diffusion", "Etest", "agar dilution"). Expected values based NCBI/EBI antibiogram specification. Note NCBI allows "MIC" synonym "broth dilution" function convert "MIC" "broth dilution" import. platform: AST platform/instrument (e.g., "Vitek", "Phoenix", "Sensititre"). guideline: AST standard recorded input file used AST assay. pheno_eucast: phenotype newly interpreted EUCAST human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_clsi: phenotype newly interpreted CLSI human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. ecoff: phenotype newly interpreted ECOFF (WT/NWT), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_provided: original phenotype interpretation provided input file. source: source data point (renamed BioProject input file, replaced single value passed source parameter).

what: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from common sources — import_pheno
title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from common sources — import_pheno
text: function imports antibiotic susceptibility testing datasets formats exported EBI, NCBI, WHOnet several automated AST instruments (Vitek, Microscan, Sensititre, Phoenix). optionally can use AMR package interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) based EUCAST CLSI guidelines (human breakpoints /ECOFF).

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from common sources — import_pheno
code: import_pheno(   input,   format = "ebi",   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE,   ... )

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from common sources — import_pheno
text: input string representing dataframe, path input file, containing phenotype data supported format. files may downloaded public sources EBI AMR web browser, EBI FTP site, NCBI browser, using functions download_ebi(), download_ncbi_pheno(), query_ncbi_bq_geno(); files may exported supported AST instruments. format string indicating format data: "ebi" (default), "ebi_web", "ebi_ftp", "ncbi", "ncbi_biosample", "vitek", "microscan", "phoenix", "sensititre", "whonet". determines importer function data passed processing (see ). interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class 'sir'. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class 'sir'. interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class 'sir' coded NWT (nonwildtype) WT (wildtype). ... Format-specific arguments. See "ebi" : import_ebi_pheno() "ebi_web" : import_ebi_pheno() "ebi_ftp" :import_ebi_pheno_ftp() "ncbi" : import_ncbi_pheno() "ncbi_biosample" : import_ncbi_biosample() "vitek" : import_vitek_pheno() "microscan" : import_microscan_pheno() "sensititre" : import_sensititre_pheno() "phoenix" : import_phoenix_pheno() "whonet" : import_whonet_pheno()

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from common sources — import_pheno
text: data frame processed AST data, including additional columns: id: sample identifier (character). spp_pheno: species phenotype, formatted using AMR::.mo() function (class mo). drug: antibiotic used test, formatted using AMR::.ab() function (class ab). mic: minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) value, formatted using AMR::.mic() function (class mic). disk: disk diffusion measurement (mm), formatted using AMR::.disk() function (class disk). method: AST method (e.g., "broth dilution", "disk diffusion", "Etest", "agar dilution"). Expected values based NCBI/EBI antibiogram specification (character). platform: AST platform/instrument (e.g., "Vitek", "Phoenix", "Sensititre") (character). guideline: AST standard recorded input file used AST assay (character). pheno_eucast: phenotype newly interpreted EUCAST human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data (class sir). pheno_clsi: phenotype newly interpreted CLSI human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data (class sir). ecoff: phenotype newly interpreted ECOFF (WT/NWT), based MIC disk diffusion data (class sir). pheno_provided: original phenotype interpretation provided input file, formatted using AMR::.sir() (class sir). source: source data point (publications bioproject field input file, replaced single value passed source parameter) (character).

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from common sources — import_pheno
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # import NCBI data retrieved from Google Cloud, without re-interpreting resistance head(staph_pheno_ncbi_cloud_raw) pheno <- import_pheno(staph_pheno_ncbi_cloud_raw, format = "ncbi")  # import NCBI data where biosample column has been renamed to 'id' head(staph_pheno_ncbi_raw) import_pheno(staph_pheno_ncbi_raw, "ncbi", sample_col = "id")  # import NCBI data and re-interpret resistance (S/I/R) and WT/NWT (vs ECOFF) head(ecoli_pheno_raw) pheno <- import_pheno(ecoli_pheno_raw,   format = "ncbi",   interpret_eucast = TRUE, interpret_ecoff = TRUE )  # download Klebsiella quasipneumoniae phenotype data from NCBI BioSample kquasi_raw_ncbi <- download_ncbi_pheno("Klebsiella quasipneumoniae") head(kquasi_raw_ncbi) # import the data and interpret against EUCAST breakpoints pheno <- import_pheno(kquasi_raw_ncbi,   format = "ncbi_biosample",   interpret_eucast = T )  # download Klebsiella quasipneumoniae phenotype data from EBI kquasi_raw_ebi <- download_ebi(species = "Klebsiella quasipneumoniae") head(kquasi_raw_ebi) # import the data and interpret against ecoff pheno <- import_pheno(kquasi_raw_ebi,   format = "ebi_ftp",   interpret_ecoff = TRUE )  # import Vitek data from file, with default parameters pheno <- import_pheno("vitek_export.tsv",   format = "vitek" )  # import Vitek data from file # specify guideline that was used, remove dates, ignore expertized calls pheno <- import_pheno("vitek_export.tsv",   format = "vitek",   instrument_guideline = "EUCAST 2025",   use_expertized = FALSE,   include_dates = FALSE ) } # }

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from BD Phoenix instruments — import_phoenix_pheno
code: import_phoenix_pheno(   input,   sample_col = NULL,   drug_col = NULL,   mic_col = NULL,   sir_col = NULL,   species_col = NULL,   species = NULL,   ab = NULL,   use_expertized = TRUE,   source = NULL,   instrument_guideline = NULL,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from BD Phoenix instruments — import_phoenix_pheno
text: input Path Phoenix export file (XLS, XLSX, TXT, TSV), data frame. sample_col Name 1-based column index sample ID column. NULL (default), auto-detected common names "Sample", "ID", "Isolate"). single-isolate files sample column, filename used sample ID. drug_col Name 1-based column index drug/antimicrobial column. NULL (default), auto-detected names "Antimicrobial", "Drug", etc. mic_col Name 1-based column index MIC column. NULL(default), auto-detected names "MIC", "MIC Concentration"`, etc. sir_col Name 1-based column index S//R interpretation column. NULL (default), auto-detected based use_expertized: prefers "Final (SIR)", "Expert (SIR)" TRUE; prefers "Interp" FALSE. species_col Name 1-based column index species/organism column. NULL (default), auto-detected names "Organism", "Species", etc. Ignored species supplied. species Optional fixed species string applied rows (parsed via AMR::.mo()). Overrides species_col. Required files without organism column. ab Optional antibiotic filter/override passed interpret_pheno(). use_expertized TRUE (default), prefer expert/final interpretation column raw instrument interpretation present sir_col specified. source Optional source label recorded source output column. instrument_guideline Optional guideline string recorded guideline column (e.g. "CLSI 2018", "EUCAST 2024"). interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function re-interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) observation based MIC values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class sir. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function re-interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) observation based MIC values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class sir. interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function re-interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status observation based MIC values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class sir coded NWT (nonwildtype) WT (wildtype).

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from BD Phoenix instruments — import_phoenix_pheno
text: Standardised long-format AST data frame

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from BD Phoenix instruments — import_phoenix_pheno
text: Phoenix instruments can export data different file formats. function handles named-header exports (e.g. tab-delimited CLSI reports columns "Antimicrobial", "MIC Concentration", "Final (SIR)") positional/headerless exports (XLS files column headers). Column detection automatic based common Phoenix header name patterns, column can overridden supplying name 1-based index via drug_col, mic_col, sir_col, sample_col, species_col. headerless files (detected standard column names recognised), function assumes standard Phoenix positional layout: column 1 = sample ID, column 2 = species, column 3 = drug, column 4 = MIC, column 5 = instrument interpretation, column 6 = expert/final interpretation. Drug names standardised using AMR::.ab(), handles abbreviated, full, language-specific names (e.g. "Fosfomycin mit G6P"). Species names standardised using AMR::.mo(). tab-delimited files, trailing non-data sections commonly appended Phoenix (e.g. "Resistance Markers", "Expert Triggered Rules") automatically discarded.

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from BD Phoenix instruments — import_phoenix_pheno
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Named-header export (e.g. CLSI report, auto-detected) pheno <- import_phoenix_pheno("phoenix_clsi_report.txt",   species = "Escherichia coli", instrument_guideline = "CLSI 2018" )  # Positional/headerless XLS export (auto-detected) pheno <- import_phoenix_pheno("phoenix_export.xls")  # Override column detection when names differ from defaults pheno <- import_phoenix_pheno("phoenix_export.xlsx",   drug_col = "Antibiotic", mic_col = "Result",   sir_col = "SIR_final", sample_col = "IsolateID",   species_col = "Organism" ) } # }

what: Import and Process Resistance Gene Identifier (RGI) Results — import_rgi
title: Import and Process Resistance Gene Identifier (RGI) Results — import_rgi
text: function imports processes genotyping results Resistance Gene Identifier (RGI, https://github.com/arpcard/rgi), extracting antimicrobial resistance determinants mapping standardised drug classes/antibiotics.

title: Import and Process Resistance Gene Identifier (RGI) Results — import_rgi
code: import_rgi(   input_table,   orf_id_col = "ORF_ID",   sample_id_sep = ".fasta.txt:",   model_col = "Model_type",   antibiotic_col = "Antibiotic",   class_col = "Drug Class",   exclude_loose = TRUE,   rgi_short_name = rgi_short_name_table,   rgi_drugs = rgi_drugs_table,   samples_no_amr = NULL )

title: Import and Process Resistance Gene Identifier (RGI) Results — import_rgi
text: input_table character string specifying dataframe path RGI results table (TSV format). orf_id_col character string specifying column identifies open reading frame ID (ORF_ID) dataset (default ORF_ID). column includes sample ID contig / genomic location default output RGI. sample_id_sep character string specifying separator sample ID separated remaining text ORF_ID (Default: .fasta.txt:) . example: ORF_ID column, "SAMEA3498968.fasta.txt:1_96 # 109511 # 110635....", sample ID separator .fasta.txt:. model_col character string specifying column identifies model type identified RGI (default Model_type). antibiotic_col Character string specifying antibiotic column (default Antibiotic). class_col Character string specifying drug class column (default Drug Class). exclude_loose Logical indicating whether exclude Loose hits (AMR markers fall curated bitscore cutoff defined CARD/RGI). Default TRUE, excludes Loose hits. rgi_short_name tibble containing reference table mapping model IDs (CARD/RGI) shortened model names provided CARD (https://card.mcmaster.ca/download aro_index.tsv). Defaults rgi_short_name_table, provided internally. rgi_drugs tibble containing reference table mapping CARD drug class / drug agents standardised drug classes/names. Defaults rgi_drugs_table, provided internally. samples_no_amr vector sample IDs RGI output AMR markers identified. example c("SampleA", "SampleB"). (default = NULL)

title: Import and Process Resistance Gene Identifier (RGI) Results — import_rgi
text: tibble containing processed AMR determinants drug classes AMRgen compatible. output retains original columns RGI output along newly mapped variables.

title: Import and Process Resistance Gene Identifier (RGI) Results — import_rgi
text: function performs following steps: Reads RGI output table. Transforms RGI output long form (.e., one AMR determinant drug class / antibiotic per row). Maps CARD drug classes antibiotics standardised names. processing ensures compatibility downstream AMRgen analysis workflows.

what: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility data from SIRscan (Bio-Rad) — import_sirscan_pheno
title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility data from SIRscan (Bio-Rad) — import_sirscan_pheno
text: SIRscan (Bio-Rad) exports AST results three separate semicolon-delimited CSV files: MIC values (CMI), disk diffusion diameters (DIAM), SIR interpretations (INTERPR). file shares row structure (one row per isolate) antibiotic columns. function reads one files, pivots long format, returns standardised AMRgen data frame.

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility data from SIRscan (Bio-Rad) — import_sirscan_pheno
code: import_sirscan_pheno(   mic_file = NULL,   disk_file = NULL,   interpr_file = NULL,   source = NULL,   species = NULL,   ab = NULL,   instrument_guideline = NULL,   sirscan_codes = sirscan_codes,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility data from SIRscan (Bio-Rad) — import_sirscan_pheno
text: mic_file Path SIRscan CMI (MIC) export file. Optional. disk_file Path SIRscan DIAM (disk diffusion) export file. Optional. interpr_file Path SIRscan INTERPR (SIR interpretation) export file. Optional. least one mic_file, disk_file, interpr_file must supplied. source Optional source label source output column. species Optional fixed species string applied rows (parsed via AMR::.mo()). NULL, Germe column file used. ab Optional antibiotic filter/override passed interpret_pheno(). instrument_guideline Optional guideline string guideline column (e.g. "EUCAST 2023"). sirscan_codes data frame mapping SIRscan antibiotic codes (sirscan_code) antibiotic names (ab_name) recognised AMR::.ab(). Defaults built-sirscan_codes table. Supply data frame override mappings — example SIRscan configuration uses S, K, C different agents defaults. Set NULL skip lookup pass codes directly AMR::.ab(). interpret_eucast Interpret MIC/disk values EUCAST human breakpoints (default FALSE). interpret_clsi Interpret MIC/disk values CLSI human breakpoints (default FALSE). interpret_ecoff Interpret MIC/disk values ECOFF values (default FALSE).

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility data from SIRscan (Bio-Rad) — import_sirscan_pheno
text: standardised long-format data frame columns: id, drug, mic, disk, pheno_provided, pheno_eucast, pheno_clsi, ecoff, method, platform, guideline, source, spp_pheno, collection_date, specimen_type.

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility data from SIRscan (Bio-Rad) — import_sirscan_pheno
text: Files 4-row metadata header (institution, lab, export date, period) followed semicolon-delimited data block. Latin-1 (Windows-1252) encoding assumed, standard French-locale SIRscan exports.

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility data from SIRscan (Bio-Rad) — import_sirscan_pheno
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Import all three SIRscan export files pheno <- import_sirscan_pheno(   mic_file     = "CMI_SALMO.csv",   disk_file    = "DIAM_SALMO.csv",   interpr_file = "INTERPR_SALMO.csv" )  # MIC + interpretations only, with EUCAST re-interpretation pheno <- import_sirscan_pheno(   mic_file = "CMI_SALMO.csv",   interpr_file = "INTERPR_SALMO.csv",   species = "Salmonella enterica",   interpret_eucast = TRUE ) } # }

what: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_pheno
title: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_pheno
text: function applies human EUCAST CLSI breakpoints, /ECOFF, interpret antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data.

title: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_pheno
code: interpret_pheno(   ast,   interpret_ecoff = TRUE,   interpret_eucast = TRUE,   interpret_clsi = TRUE,   species = NULL,   ab = NULL )

title: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_pheno
text: ast tibble containing phenotype measures standard AMRgen format, output import_pheno(). must contain assay measurements columns mic (class mic) /disk (class disk). Interpretation requires organism (column spp_pheno class mo, single value passed via species parameter) antibiotic (column drug class ab, single value passed via ab parameter). interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class sir coded NWT (nonwildtype) WT (wildtype). interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class sir. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class sir. species (optional) Name species use phenotype interpretation. default, spp_pheno field input file assumed specify species sample, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single species name via parameter. ab (optional) Name antibiotic use phenotype interpretation. default, drug field input file assumed specify antibiotic sample, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single antibiotic name via parameter.

title: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_pheno
text: copy input table, additional columns: pheno_eucast: phenotype newly interpreted EUCAST human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_clsi: phenotype newly interpreted CLSI human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. ecoff: phenotype newly interpreted ECOFF (WT/NWT), based MIC disk diffusion data. spp_pheno: species phenotype, formatted using AMR::.mo() function (either taken field spp_pheno input table, single value specified species parameter). drug: antibiotic used test, formatted using AMR::.ab() function (either taken field drug input table, single value specified ab parameter).

title: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_pheno
code: # import without re-interpreting resistance pheno <- import_ncbi_pheno(ecoli_pheno_raw) #> Warning: Expected AST method column 'Laboratory typing method' not found in input #> Warning: Expected column 'BioProject' not found in input head(pheno) #> # A tibble: 6 × 29 #>   id    drug    mic  disk guideline method  platform pheno_provided spp_pheno    #>   <chr> <ab>  <mic> <dsk> <chr>     <chr>   <chr>    <sir>          <mo>         #> 1 SAMN… CIP  256.00    NA CLSI      broth … NA         R            B_ESCHR_COLI #> 2 SAMN… CIP   64.00    NA CLSI      Etest   Etest      R            B_ESCHR_COLI #> 3 SAMN… CIP  >=4.00    NA CLSI      broth … NA         R            B_ESCHR_COLI #> 4 SAMN… CIP  >=4.00    NA CLSI      broth … NA         R            B_ESCHR_COLI #> 5 SAMN… CIP  <=0.25    NA CLSI      broth … NA         S            B_ESCHR_COLI #> 6 SAMN… CIP  >=4.00    NA CLSI      broth … NA         R            B_ESCHR_COLI #> # ℹ 20 more variables: `Organism group` <chr>, `Scientific name` <chr>, #> #   `Isolation type` <chr>, Location <chr>, `Isolation source` <chr>, #> #   Isolate <chr>, Antibiotic <chr>, `Resistance phenotype` <chr>, #> #   `Measurement sign` <chr>, `MIC (mg/L)` <dbl>, `Disk diffusion (mm)` <lgl>, #> #   `Laboratory typing platform` <chr>, Vendor <chr>, #> #   `Laboratory typing method version or reagent` <chr>, #> #   `Testing standard` <chr>, `Create date` <dttm>, pheno_clsi_mic <sir>, …  # interpret phenotypes pheno <- interpret_pheno(pheno)  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pheno <- read_csv("AST.csv") %>%   # convert antibiotic field to 'drug' of class 'ab'   mutate(drug = as.ab(antibiotic)) %>%   mutate(mic = paste0(sign, MIC)) %>%   mutate(mic = as.mic(mic)) # create a single 'mic' column of class 'mic'  pheno <- interpret_pheno(pheno, species = "Escherichia coli") } # }
