what: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
title: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
text: function creates ggplot object visualizing logistic regression coefficients 95% confidence intervals. Significant markers highlighted based specified p-value threshold.

title: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
code: plot_estimates(   tbl,   sig = 0.05,   sig_colors = c(`FALSE` = "grey", `TRUE` = "blue4"),   x_title = "Coefficient (95% CI)",   y_title = "Variant",   title = NULL,   axis_label_size = 9,   marker_order = NULL )

title: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
text: tbl data frame tibble containing logistic regression results. Expected columns : marker: name marker (e.g., variable name). pval: p-value marker. ci.lower: lower bound confidence interval. ci.upper: upper bound confidence interval. est: estimated coefficient. sig (optional) significance threshold p-values. Defaults 0.05. sig_colors (optional) vector two colors represent significant non-significant estimates. x_title (optional) title x-axis. Defaults "Coefficient (95% CI)". y_title (optional) title y-axis. Defaults "Variant". title (optional) main title plot. NULL, title added. axis_label_size (optional) font size axis labels. Defaults 9. marker_order (optional) Vector indicating order markers plotted y-axis.

title: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
text: ggplot object showing logistic regression coefficients confidence intervals. Significant markers (p-value < sig) colored differently.

title: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
code: tbl <- tibble::tibble(   marker = c("(Intercept)", "var1", "var2", "var3"),   pval = c(0.1, 0.03, 0.2, 0.04),   ci.lower = c(-0.2, 0.1, -0.3, 0.2),   ci.upper = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.4, 1.1),   est = c(0.2, 0.5, 0.1, 0.7) )  plot_estimates(tbl)

title: Generate PPV Plot — ppv
text: function generates plot showing summaries phenotype results (assay distributions, phenotype category percentages, /predictive value phenotype) combination markers observed data.

title: Generate PPV Plot — ppv
code: ppv(   binary_matrix = NULL,   min_set_size = 2,   order = "ppv",   geno_table,   pheno_table,   pheno_drug = NULL,   geno_class = NULL,   geno_drug = NULL,   geno_sample_col = NULL,   pheno_sample_col = NULL,   sir_col = NULL,   ecoff_col = "ecoff",   marker_col = "marker",   colours_ppv = c(R = "maroon", NWT = "navy"),   SIR_col = c(S = "#3CAEA3", I = "#F6D55C", R = "#ED553B"),   upset_grid = FALSE,   marker_label_space = NULL,   plot_category = TRUE,   print_category_counts = TRUE,   plot_ppv = TRUE,   plot_assay = FALSE,   assay = NULL,   boxplot_col = "grey",   species = NULL,   bp_site = NULL,   guideline = "EUCAST 2025",   bp_S = NULL,   bp_R = NULL,   ecoff_bp = NULL,   pd = position_dodge(width = 0.8),   marker_order = NULL )

title: Generate PPV Plot — ppv
text: binary_matrix data frame containing original binary matrix output get_binary_matrix() function. provided (set NULL), user must specify geno_table, pheno_table, pheno_drug, optionally geno_class, geno_drug, geno_sample_col, pheno_sample_col, sir_col, ecoff_col, marker_col pass get_binary_matrix(). min_set_size integer specifying minimum size gene set included analysis plots. Default 2. marker combinations least number occurrences included plots. order character string indicating order combinations x-axis. Options : "": decreasing frequency combinations "genes": order number genes combination "value": order median assay value (MIC disk zone) combination. "ppv" (default): order PPV estimated combination geno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing genotype data, formatted import_amrfp(). used binary_matrix provided. pheno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing phenotype data, formatted import_pheno(). used binary_matrix provided. pheno_drug (Required binary_matrix provided) character string specifying drug interest filter phenotype data. value must match one entries drug column pheno_table coercible match using AMR::.ab. geno_class (Optional binary_matrix provided) character vector drug classes filter genotype markers. Markers geno_table filtered based whether drug_class matches value list. provided, AMR pkg used check class name/s associated pheno_drug uses (printed screen user can see filtered). geno_drug (Optional binary_matrix provided) character vector drug names whose relevant genotype markers included. geno_sample_col (Optional) character string specifying column name geno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. pheno_sample_col (Optional) character string specifying column name pheno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. sir_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretation (SIR) data. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). provided, first column prefixed "phenotype*" used present, otherwise error thrown.  used binary_matrix provided. ecoff_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretations (SIR) made ECOFF rather clinical breakpoint. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). Default ecoff. Set NULL available.  used binary_matrix provided. marker_col character string specifying column name geno_table containing marker identifiers. Default "marker". used binary_matrix provided. colours_ppv named vector colours plot PPV estimates. names "R", "" "NWT", values valid colour names hexadecimal colour codes. SIR_col named vector colours percentage bar plot. names phenotype categories (e.g., "R", "", "S"), values valid colour names hexadecimal colour codes. Default values used AMR package AMR::scale_colour_sir(). upset_grid Logical indicating whether show marker combinations upset plot-style grid (default FALSE, row instead labelled printed list markers). marker_label_space Relative width plotting area provide marker list/grid. (Default NULL, results default value 3 upset_grid=FALSE 1 otherwise). plot_category Logical indicating whether include stacked bar plot showing, marker combination, proportion samples phenotype classification (specified pheno column input file). Default TRUE. print_category_counts Logical indicating whether, plot_category=TRUE, print number strains resistance category marker combination plot. Default FALSE. plot_ppv Logical indicating whether plot estimates positive predictive value, marker combination (default TRUE). plot_assay Logical indicating whether plot distribution MIC/disk assay values, marker combination (default FALSE). Note must also indicate assay column plot ("mic" "disk") via assay. assay character string indicating whether plot MIC disk diffusion data. Must one : NULL: (default) assay data plotted "mic": plot MIC data stored column mic "disk": plot disk diffusion data stored column disk boxplot_col Colour lines box plots summarising MIC distribution marker combination. Default "grey". species (Optional) Species name used retrieve clinical breakpoints annotation assay distribution plot. bp_site (Optional) Breakpoint site (e.g. "Non-meningitis") used retrieving clinical breakpoints. guideline Guideline used breakpoint lookup. Default "EUCAST 2025". bp_S (Optional) S breakpoint add assay distribution plot (numerical). bp_R (Optional) R breakpoint add assay distribution plot (numerical). ecoff_bp (Optional) ECOFF breakpoint add assay distribution plot (numerical). pd ggplot2::position_dodge() object controlling horizontal spacing points confidence intervals PPV plot. Default position_dodge(width = 0.8). marker_order (optional) Force order markers upset grid. Default NULL case markers ordered decreasing order freauency.

title: Generate PPV Plot — ppv
text: list containing following elements: plot: grid requested plots binary_matrix: copy genotype-phenotype binary matrix (either provided input generated function) summary: data frame summarizing marker combination observed, including number resistant isolates, positive predictive values, median assay values (interquartile range) relevant.

title: Generate PPV Plot — ppv
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ecoli_geno <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name")  # Generate binary matrix binary_matrix <- get_binary_matrix(   geno_table = ecoli_geno,   pheno_table = ecoli_pheno,   pheno_drug = "Ciprofloxacin",   geno_class = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "pheno_clsi",   keep_assay_values = TRUE,   keep_assay_values_from = "mic" )  # Run ppv analysis using this binary_matrix ppv <- ppv(binary_matrix)  # Alternatively, generate binary matrix and run ppv() in one step ppv <- ppv(   geno_table = ecoli_geno,   pheno_table = ecoli_pheno,   pheno_drug = "Ciprofloxacin",   sir_col = "pheno_clsi" ) } # }

title: SIRscan antibiotic code mapping — sirscan_codes
text: Confirmed codes (verified Bio-Rad catalogue collaborator communication): CF30 (cephalothin 30 mcg disk), MA (cefamandole), SSS (sulfonamides), SXT25 (trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole 25 mcg disk). Codes mapped French CASFM/SIRscan convention, yet confirmed official Bio-Rad documentation: S (streptomycin), K (kanamycin), C (chloramphenicol), NALI (nalidixic acid), AZ (azithromycin), MM (minocycline), ERM (erythromycin). Codes confirmed mapping (CAN, ECM, ICM) absent table; return NA AMR::.ab() warning. Update table supply correct mappings known.

what: Perform Solo PPV Analysis for AMR Markers — solo_ppv
title: Perform Solo PPV Analysis for AMR Markers — solo_ppv
text: function performs Positive Predictive Value (PPV) analysis AMR markers associated given drug genotype marker filter. calculates PPV solo markers visualises results using various plots.

title: Perform Solo PPV Analysis for AMR Markers — solo_ppv
code: solo_ppv(   geno_table,   pheno_table,   pheno_drug = NULL,   geno_class = NULL,   geno_drug = NULL,   geno_sample_col = NULL,   pheno_sample_col = NULL,   sir_col = NULL,   ecoff_col = "ecoff",   icat = FALSE,   marker_col = "marker",   reverse_order = FALSE,   binary_matrix = NULL,   min = 1,   axis_label_size = 9,   pd = position_dodge(width = 0.8),   excludeRanges = NULL,   colours_SIR = c(S = "#3CAEA3", SDD = "#8FD6C4", I = "#F6D55C", R = "#ED553B"),   colours_ppv = c(R = "maroon", I = "skyblue", NWT = "navy") )

title: Perform Solo PPV Analysis for AMR Markers — solo_ppv
text: geno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing genotype data, formatted import_amrfp(). used binary_matrix provided. pheno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing phenotype data, formatted import_pheno(). used binary_matrix provided. pheno_drug (Required binary_matrix provided) character string specifying drug interest filter phenotype data. value must match one entries drug column pheno_table coercible match using AMR::.ab. geno_class (Optional binary_matrix provided) character vector drug classes filter genotype markers. Markers geno_table filtered based whether drug_class matches value list. provided, AMR pkg used check class name/s associated pheno_drug uses (printed screen user can see filtered). geno_drug (Optional binary_matrix provided) character vector drug names whose relevant genotype markers included. geno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name geno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. pheno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name pheno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. sir_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretation (SIR) data. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). provided, first column prefixed "phenotype*" used present, otherwise error thrown.  used binary_matrix provided. ecoff_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains ECOFF interpretation phenotype. values interpretable "WT" (wildtype) "NWT" (nonwildtype), "S" / "" / "R". Default ecoff. Set NULL available.  used binary_matrix provided. icat logical indicating whether calculate PPV "" (category exists phenotype column) (default FALSE). marker_col character string specifying column name geno_table containing marker identifiers. Default "marker". used binary_matrix provided. reverse_order logical indicating whether reverse order rows plot, markers ordered lowest R PPV highest (default FALSE, .e. markers ordered highest lowest PPV). binary_matrix data frame containing original binary matrix output get_binary_matrix() function. provided (set NULL), user must specify geno_table, pheno_table, pheno_drug, optionally geno_class, geno_drug, geno_sample_col, pheno_sample_col, sir_col, ecoff_col, marker_col pass get_binary_matrix(). min Minimum number genomes solo marker, include marker plot (default 1). axis_label_size Font size axis labels PPV plot (default 9). pd ggplot2::position_dodge() object controlling horizontal spacing points confidence intervals PPV plot. Default position_dodge(width = 0.8). excludeRanges Vector phenotype categories (comprised "R", "", "NWT") ignore MIC values expressed ranges calculating PPVs. include MICs expressed ranges set NULL. colours_SIR named vector colours percentage bar plot. names phenotype categories (e.g., "R", "", "S"), values valid colour names hexadecimal colour codes. Default values used AMR package AMR::scale_fill_sir(). colours_ppv named vector colours plot PPV estimates. names "R", "", "NWT", values valid colour names hexadecimal colour codes.

title: Perform Solo PPV Analysis for AMR Markers — solo_ppv
text: list containing following elements: solo_stats: data frame summarizing PPV resistance (R vs S/) NWT (R/vs S), including number positive hits, sample size, PPV, 95% confidence intervals marker. combined_plot: combined ggplot object showing PPV plot solo markers, bar plot phenotype distribution. solo_binary: dataframe binary values indicating presence absence solo markers. solo_binary_norange: dataframe binary values indicating presence absence solo markers, excluding samples MIC disk measures expressed ranges. amr_binary: copy genotype-phenotype binary matrix markers (either provided input generated function) plot_order: Ordered list rows output plot (facilitate alignment plots)

title: Perform Solo PPV Analysis for AMR Markers — solo_ppv
text: function analyses predictive power individual AMR markers found 'solo' genome markers associated class. phenotype data matched genotype presence/absence stratified compute PPV resistance non-wild-type interpretations. function also generates plots aid interpretation.

title: Perform Solo PPV Analysis for AMR Markers — solo_ppv
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ geno_table <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name") head(geno_table)  # Generate binary matrix binary_matrix <- get_binary_matrix(   geno_table = geno_table,   pheno_table = ecoli_pheno,   pheno_drug = "Ciprofloxacin",   geno_class = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "pheno_clsi",   keep_assay_values = TRUE,   keep_assay_values_from = "mic" )  # Run solo PPV analysis plot analysis using this binary_matrix # (note pheno_drug / geno_class are optional here, and only used # for titling the plot) soloPPV_cipro <- solo_ppv(binary_matrix = binary_matrix)  soloPPV_cipro$solo_stats soloPPV_cipro$combined_plot } # }

Name column containing marker identifiers. Default "marker". drug_col Character. Name column containing drug agent identifiers. Name column containing variation type identifiers. Default "variation type". force_ab Logical. TRUE, attempts convert entries drug_col antibiotic names using AMR::.ab even column class "ab" Default FALSE.

Number indicates count hits detected per marker.

text: geno_table tibble data frame containing genotype data, format output import_amrfp. pheno_table tibble data frame containing phenotype data, format output import_pheno. geno_sample_col Character. Name column genotype table containing sample identifiers. Default "Name". pheno_sample_col Character. Name column phenotype table containing sample identifiers. Default "id". pheno_cols Vector. Vector giving names columns phenotype table containing categorical phenotype calls (S//R NWT/WT). Default columns beginning "pheno" "ecoff". drug_col Character. Name column genotype table containing drug class identifiers. Name column genotype table containing marker identifiers. Default "marker". gene_col Character. Name column genotype table containing gene identifiers. Default "gene". variation_col Character. Name column genotype table containing variation type identifiers. Default "variation type". mic_col Character. Name column phenotype table containing MIC measurements Default "mic". disk_col Character. Name column phenotype table containing disk diffusion zone measurements. Default "disk". spp_col Character. Name column phenotype table containing species names. Default "spp_pheno". method_cols Vector. Vector giving names columns phenotype table containing method source information summarise MIC/disk data. Default c("method", "platform", "guideline", "source").

title: Summarise a Phenotype Table — summarise_pheno
text: summarise_pheno() computes summary information phenotype table.

title: Summarise a Phenotype Table — summarise_pheno
code: summarise_pheno(   pheno_table,   sample_col = "id",   drug_col = "drug",   mic_col = "mic",   disk_col = "disk",   spp_col = "spp_pheno",   pheno_cols = NULL,   method_cols = c("method", "platform", "guideline", "source"),   force_ab = FALSE )

title: Summarise a Phenotype Table — summarise_pheno
text: pheno_table tibble data frame containing phenotype data, format output import_pheno. sample_col Character. Name column containing sample identifiers. Default "id". drug_col Character. Name column containing drug agent identifiers. Default "drug". class 'ab' entries annotated full antibiotic names, converted using AMR::.ab. desired behaviour class 'ab', set force_ab=TRUE. mic_col Character. Name column containing MIC measurements Default "mic". disk_col Character. Name column containing disk diffusion zone measurements. Default "disk". spp_col Character. Name column containing species names. Default "spp_pheno". pheno_cols Vector. Vector giving names columns containing categorical phenotype calls (S//R NWT/WT). Default columns beginning "pheno" "ecoff". method_cols Vector. Vector giving names columns containing method source information summarise MIC/disk data. Default c("method", "platform", "guideline", "source"). force_ab Logical. TRUE, attempts convert entries drug_col antibiotic names using AMR::.ab even column class "ab" Default FALSE.

title: Summarise a Phenotype Table — summarise_pheno
text: named list following elements: uniques: tibble number unique samples, drugs, organisms, methods detected pheno_table. drugs: tibble listing drugs included table, associated number samples MIC measures, disk measures, neither , drug species. details: tibble listing details methods assay measurements, per drug species. pheno_counts_list: list tibbles, corresponding unique categorical phenotype column input, indicating counts phenotypic category per drug species.

title: Summarise a Phenotype Table — summarise_pheno
text: function automatically adapts presence absence columns pheno_table. force_ab parameter allows addition full antibiotic names using ab_name() function even first column recognized "ab" object.
